top5Opportunities.statement: Minimize the number of profile units with undetected dimensional drift > IT9 within a delivery batch that propagates into failed subassembly geometry
Minimize the likelihood of qualifying a profile supplier that cannot produce EN 10204 Type 3.1 material test reports required for CE-marked construction equipment
Increase the ability to complete full first-article qualification of a custom structural steel profile within 8 weeks of tool delivery
Minimize the production downtime caused by a profile batch rejection requiring incoming re-inspection, quarantine, and replacement ordering within a Kanban-scheduled fabrication cell
Minimize supply disruption risk from single-source dependency on a custom-profile supplier for annual volumes > 500 machine-sets

needs.statement: Minimize the number of profile units with undetected dimensional drift > IT9 (+-0.043 mm) within a delivery batch that propagates into failed subassembly geometry, discovered only at machine final assembly inspection
needs.importanceRationale: Plate fab dimensions depend on operator, fixture wear, weld sequence, and thermal history — dimensional drift is systemic and hard to detect without per-unit CMM. Cold-drawn IT8 profiles deliver die-controlled consistency.

needs.statement: Minimize the likelihood of qualifying a profile supplier that cannot produce EN 10204 Type 3.1 material test reports required for structural members in CE-marked construction equipment
needs.importanceRationale: EN 10204 3.1 is mandatory for CE-marked structural components. Not all profile competitors deliver this as standard.

needs.statement: Increase the ability to complete full first-article qualification of a custom structural steel profile (dimensional, EN 10204 3.1 material cert, surface hardness) within 8 weeks of tool delivery
needs.importanceRationale: FAI cycle time directly determines platform development schedule.

needs.statement: Minimize the production downtime caused by a profile batch rejection requiring incoming re-inspection, quarantine decision, and replacement ordering within a Kanban-scheduled structural fabrication cell
needs.importanceRationale: Batch rejection halts the Kanban cell and propagates schedule delay through the machine build sequence.

needs.statement: Minimize supply disruption risk from single-source dependency on a custom-profile supplier for a structural component geometry produced in annual volumes > 500 machine-sets
needs.importanceRationale: Custom-profile geometry requires geometry-specific die — inherently single-source; no second supplier can produce without duplicate die investment (~5-15K EUR + 6-12 wk).
